market_positioning.short_term_view_1_5d: Broad participation is decisively weak, so the higher-probability 1-5 day edge is selective momentum in energy and commodities while keeping overall exposure defensive and avoiding aggressive dip-buying.
market_positioning.swing_view: Secondary swing setups favor narrow real-asset leadership and tactical oversold bounce watchlists, but weak breadth and stressed bonds/credit argue against broad swing longs.
reversal_themes: utilities and real estate oversold rebound watchlist
supporting_signals: Breadth is extremely weak with 4 advancers versus 44 decliners.
Average 1-day return is deeply negative at roughly -1.89%.
Average volume and range ratios are elevated, confirming broad downside participation and volatility expansion.
Energy and commodity ETFs with positive 3-day momentum include XLE, USO, DBC, PDBC, CORN and DBA.
Industry momentum is dominated by oil, gas and coal groups, confirming narrow real-asset leadership.
Treasuries, aggregate bonds and credit are all down, so cross-asset conditions are stressed rather than supportive.
conflicting_signals: Positive 3-day momentum exists in commodities and energy despite weak equity breadth.
Several mean-reversion lists are populated by deeply oversold assets, increasing short-covering bounce risk.
Some leadership is narrow rather than absent, so outright blanket short exposure is less attractive than selective positioning.

evidence: XLE is positive over 3 and 5 days.
USO shows strong positive 1-day and 3-day follow-through.
Industry momentum is dominated by oil & gas E&P, integrated, refining, equipment and midstream.
allocation_buckets.risk_or_invalidation: Fails if XLE and USO lose short-term relative strength or if energy stops outperforming while broad risk stays weak.

evidence: DBC and PDBC both have positive 3-day and 5-day returns.
CORN and DBA are on the positive 3-day momentum list.
Leadership outside equities is concentrated in real-asset exposure.
allocation_buckets.risk_or_invalidation: Reduce if DBC/PDBC lose positive 3-day momentum or if commodity leadership narrows further to only one contract.

evidence: SPY, EFA, IEFA, EWJ and EEM all show sharp recent downside pressure.
Negative average return with elevated volume and range supports continuation of weak tape.
allocation_buckets.risk_or_invalidation: Invalidated by breadth stabilization and a clear expansion in advancers, especially if index ETFs stop making downside progress.

evidence: XLU and XLRE have severe 1-day, 3-day and 5-day losses.
Long-duration Treasuries are also weak, reinforcing pressure on rate-sensitive groups.
These sectors appear on mean-reversion lists only after already suffering heavy downside.
allocation_buckets.risk_or_invalidation: Higher squeeze risk after sharp drops; reduce if TLT stabilizes and utilities/real estate stop underperforming.

allocation_buckets.theme: oversold precious metals and bond rebound watchlist
evidence: Silver, gold and related mining groups dominate industry mean-reversion.
TLT, TIP, AGG and BND are on ETF mean-reversion lists after sharp declines.
The moves are extended enough to allow tactical rebounds if selling pressure pauses.
allocation_buckets.risk_or_invalidation: These are watchlists, not core longs; invalidate if bonds and metals continue making fresh downside extensions without stabilization.

evidence: Bond and credit weakness means there is no clean defensive refuge.
Volatility and range expansion argue for lower gross exposure.
Narrow leadership in energy/commodities is not enough to justify aggressive net long positioning.
allocation_buckets.risk_or_invalidation: Scale down only if breadth materially improves and leadership broadens beyond real assets.
